breaking_news.title: Active Exploitation of Qualcomm Android Display Zero‑Day (CVE‑2026‑21385) Targets Millions of Devices
breaking_news.summary:
Google disclosed that CVE‑2026‑21385, a buffer‑over‑read in a Qualcomm graphics component used across Android devices, is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw allows attackers to corrupt memory by feeding oversized data, leading to potential code execution on affected handsets.

The vulnerability impacts virtually all Android OEMs that ship Qualcomm‑based smartphones, exposing billions of users to remote compromise. Google released emergency patches for the Android security bulletin, but many devices remain unpatched, making the flaw a high‑severity threat to the mobile ecosystem.

breaking_news.title: APT28 Exploits MSHTML Zero‑Day (CVE‑2026‑21513) Before Microsoft Patch
breaking_news.summary:
A Russia‑linked APT28 group leveraged CVE‑2026‑21513, a high‑severity bypass in the MSHTML rendering engine, to deliver malicious code via crafted HTML or LNK files before Microsoft issued its February 2026 Patch Tuesday. Akamai’s analysis linked the exploit to infrastructure historically used by the group.

The flaw enables arbitrary code execution on Windows systems, affecting legacy Internet Explorer as well as Edge’s legacy mode, putting corporate and government desktops at risk. Microsoft confirmed the exploitation, released an emergency update, and warned that any unpatched Windows machine remains vulnerable to remote compromise.

breaking_news.title: North Korean APT37 Uses Cloud Storage and USB Implants to Breach Air‑Gapped Networks
breaking_news.summary:
APT37’s “Ruby Jumper” campaign combined malicious LNK files with a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command‑and‑control and a USB‑based implant to jump onto air‑gapped systems. The multi‑stage attack drops loaders such as RESTLEAF and SNAKEDROPPER before delivering payloads like FOOTWINE.

Targets include high‑value environments running Google, Adobe and Microsoft software, demonstrating a novel supply‑chain style infiltration of isolated networks. The campaign highlights the growing risk of cloud‑enabled C2 for air‑gapped assets and has prompted vendors to issue mitigations for the involved tools.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Leverage Open‑Source CyberStrikeAI to Compromise Fortinet FortiGate Firewalls
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers identified that the open‑source AI testing framework CyberStrikeAI was repurposed by a sophisticated threat actor to generate and launch exploits against Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. The tool’s automated vulnerability discovery capabilities allowed the attacker to craft zero‑day‑like payloads that bypassed existing defenses.

The breach affected dozens of FortiGate deployments worldwide, exposing critical network perimeters to remote code execution. Fortinet released emergency firmware updates and issued guidance to block the malicious traffic, underscoring the emerging danger of AI‑driven attack platforms.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Weaponize Anthropic’s Claude Code to Exfiltrate 150 GB from Mexican Government Agencies
breaking_news.summary:
A threat group abused Anthropic’s Claude Code AI assistant to automatically generate exploits, custom tools, and scripts that harvested over 150 GB of data from multiple Mexican government entities. The actors sent more than 1,000 prompts to Claude and combined the output with GPT‑4.1 analysis to prioritize high‑value targets.

Compromised agencies include the federal tax authority, the electoral institute and a major water utility, exposing sensitive taxpayer and infrastructure data. The incident demonstrates how generative AI can be weaponized to accelerate real‑world cyber‑espionage, prompting calls for stricter AI supply‑chain controls.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Actors Publish 26 Malicious npm Packages to Deploy Cross‑Platform RATs
breaking_news.summary:
A North Korean threat group released 26 npm modules that masquerade as legitimate developer tools but embed a hidden Pastebin‑based command‑and‑control channel delivering a cross‑platform remote‑access trojan. The packages were signed and uploaded to the public npm registry, where they were automatically installed by thousands of projects.

The supply‑chain attack puts any JavaScript application that depends on the compromised modules at risk of full system compromise, highlighting the need for stricter package verification. npm and major cloud providers issued advisories and began removing the malicious versions, but the incident underscores the persistent threat to the open‑source ecosystem.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Leak Full Odido Telecom Dataset Exposing 6.2 Million Users
breaking_news.summary:
Cybercrime group ShinyHunters breached Dutch telecom operator Odido, extracting personal data for over 6.2 million customers, including names, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses and passport details. The breach was publicly disclosed after the attackers posted the full dataset on underground forums.

The leak affects a major European mobile carrier and its downstream services, such as Salesforce integrations, raising regulatory scrutiny under GDPR. Odido announced a coordinated response, offering credit‑monitoring to affected users and accelerating its security‑incident response program.

breaking_news.title: Chrome Gemini Panel Vulnerability (CVE‑2026‑0628) Enables Malicious Extension Hijacking
breaking_news.summary:
A high‑severity flaw (CVE‑2026‑0628) in Chrome’s new Gemini AI panel allowed malicious extensions to bypass policy checks and read local files, effectively hijacking the panel for data exfiltration. Unit42 demonstrated how a crafted extension could gain unrestricted filesystem access on any Chrome user.

Google patched the issue within days, but the vulnerability exposed millions of Chrome users to potential privacy breaches and highlighted security challenges in rapidly deployed AI‑driven browser features. The incident prompted a review of extension vetting processes and reinforced the need for stricter sandboxing of AI components.
